headline: Transcript: The vigorous nods of Bessent
summary: Katie Martin speaks to Robert Armstrong
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Unhedged podcast episode: ‘The vigorous nods of Bessent’

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Katie MartinFor years and years, maybe a decade and a half, the most important people in financial markets were central bankers, the wise men and women who set interest rates and often also act as regulators. Today though, they kind of play second fiddle to politicians, especially in the US.

And in the US, there’s two people who really matter. Three, if you include Rob Armstrong, which I don’t. The two men that matter are first of all, of course, Mr Donald J Trump, but also his right-hand man in all things financial, Treasury secretary Scott Bessent. Now this one guy is a big reason why markets are so well behaved while policy has been so, let’s say unorthodox and Bessent knows it.

Today on the show, we’re asking what the limits of that really are and whether in the long term, you really can bring Maga to markets without causing an accident.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

This is Unhedged
